name: HTTP Webhook Client for External Integrations
description: HTTP webhook client system for integrating with external APIs and web services
author: ATHENA Team

metadata.description: Wiring diagram for HTTP webhook client with sensors

metadata.content:
# HTTP Webhook Client for External Integrations

## Overview
This template implements an HTTP webhook client system for integrating Arduino devices with external APIs and web services. It supports multiple trigger modes, data batching, and local configuration.

## Features
- **Multiple Trigger Modes**: Change, threshold, periodic, and immediate triggering
- **Data Batching**: Batch multiple sensor readings for efficiency
- **Retry Logic**: Automatic retry with exponential backoff
- **Local Server**: Web interface for configuration and monitoring
- **Multiple Sensors**: Support for digital and analog sensors
- **Manual Trigger**: Button-based manual webhook triggering
- **Status Monitoring**: LED indication and web status page
- **Flexible Integration**: Works with any HTTP endpoint

## Configuration Options
- **Wi-Fi Settings**: SSID, password for network connection
- **Webhook URL**: Primary HTTP endpoint for data
- **API Key**: Authentication token for webhook requests
- **Device ID**: Unique identifier for the client
- **Sensor Configuration**: Pin assignments, types, thresholds, units
- **Trigger Mode**: When to send webhooks (change/threshold/periodic/immediate)
- **Send Interval**: Periodic send frequency (10-3600 seconds)
- **Batching**: Enable data batching (default: enabled)
- **Batch Size**: Maximum readings per batch (1-50)
- **Retries**: Enable retry logic (default: enabled)
- **Max Retries**: Maximum retry attempts (1-10)
- **Retry Delay**: Delay between retries (1-60 seconds)
- **Local Server**: Enable configuration web server (default: enabled)
- **Server Port**: Port for local web interface (80-65535)
- **Status LED**: LED pin for status indication
- **Config Button**: Button pin for manual trigger

## Trigger Modes
### Change Mode
- Sends webhook when sensor value changes
- Configurable change threshold to avoid noise
- Best for level sensors and switches

### Threshold Mode
- Sends webhook when sensor crosses threshold value
- Only triggers on upward crossing
- Best for temperature, pressure, humidity sensors

### Periodic Mode
- Sends webhook at regular intervals
- Includes all sensor data
- Best for continuous monitoring

### Immediate Mode
- Sends webhook on every sensor reading
- High frequency, high data volume
- Best for critical events

## Webhook Payload Format
### Single Reading
```json
{
  "deviceId": "athena-webhook-001",
  "timestamp": 1234567890,
  "eventType": "sensor_reading",
  "wifiRSSI": -45,
  "ip": "192.168.1.100",
  "sensorData": {
    "name": "temperature",
    "value": 23.5,
    "unit": "°C"
  }
}
```

### Batched Readings
```json
{
  "deviceId": "athena-webhook-001",
  "timestamp": 1234567890,
  "eventType": "sensor_batch",
  "wifiRSSI": -45,
  "ip": "192.168.1.100",
  "readings": [
    {
      "timestamp": 1234567890,
      "sensorName": "temperature",
      "value": 23.5,
      "unit": "°C"
    },
    {
      "timestamp": 1234567891,
      "sensorName": "motion",
      "value": 1,
      "unit": "bool"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Local Web Interface
- **Status Page**: Real-time sensor readings and system status
- **Configuration API**: JSON endpoint for device configuration
- **Manual Trigger**: Web-based manual webhook triggering
- **Sensor Grid**: Visual display of all sensor data

### Endpoints
- `GET /` - Main status page
- `GET /config` - Device configuration (JSON)
- `GET /status` - Current status (JSON)
- `POST /trigger` - Manual webhook trigger

## Supported Sensors
### Digital Sensors
- **Motion Sensors**: PIR, microwave, ultrasonic
- **Contact Sensors**: Door/window, reed switches
- **Buttons**: Push buttons, switches
- **Level Sensors**: Water level, bin full sensors

### Analog Sensors
- **Temperature**: LM35, thermistors, PT100
- **Light**: LDR, photoresistors, BH1750
- **Humidity**: DHT11 (analog mode), capacitive
- **Pressure**: MPX5700, pressure transducers

## Integration Examples
### IFTTT Integration
```javascript
// Webhook URL: https://maker.ifttt.com/trigger/{event}/with/key/{key}
// Payload triggers IFTTT applets
```

### Zapier Integration
```javascript
// Webhook URL: https://hooks.zapier.com/hooks/catch/{id}/
// Payload can trigger Zapier workflows
```

### Custom API Integration
```javascript
// Your server endpoint
app.post('/webhook', (req, res) => {
  const { deviceId, sensorData } = req.body;
  console.log(`Device ${deviceId} reports: ${sensorData.name} = ${sensorData.value}${sensorData.unit}`);
  // Process sensor data
  res.status(200).send({success: true});
});
```

### Slack Integration
```javascript
// Incoming webhook URL for Slack
// Format payload for Slack message format
```

## LED Status Patterns
- **Solid ON**: Idle and connected to Wi-Fi
- **Fast Blink**: Sending webhook data
- **Very Fast Blink**: Connection error
- **OFF**: Disconnected from Wi-Fi

## Supported Boards
- ESP32 DevKit v1
- ESP8266 NodeMCU v2

## Dependencies
- WiFi library (built-in)
- HTTPClient library (built-in)
- ArduinoJson library v6.21.3
- EEPROM library (built-in)
- WebServer library (built-in)

## Setup Instructions
1. Connect sensors to specified pins
2. Connect status LED to pin {{.ledPin}}
3. Connect button to pin {{.buttonPin}}
4. Configure Wi-Fi and webhook settings
5. Upload sketch to device
6. Test webhook integration
7. Monitor local web interface

## Advanced Configuration
- **Custom Headers**: Add custom HTTP headers
- **TLS Support**: Enable HTTPS endpoints
- **Data Transformation**: Modify payload format
- **Multiple Webhooks**: Configure backup endpoints
- **Rate Limiting**: Respect API rate limits

## Performance Specifications
- **HTTP Request**: <500ms typical
- **Sensor Reading**: <10ms per sensor
- **Batch Processing**: <100ms for full batch
- **Memory Usage**: ~25KB for buffers
- **Power Consumption**: ~90mA (Wi-Fi active)
- **Data Rate**: Configurable based on trigger mode

## Troubleshooting
- **Webhook Not Sending**: Check URL, API key, network connectivity
- **Sensor Not Triggering**: Verify pin assignments and thresholds
- **Batch Not Working**: Check batching configuration and buffer size
- **Local Server Not Accessible**: Check IP address and port
- **LED Not Working**: Verify LED connections and pin assignment
- **Button Not Responding**: Check button wiring and pull-up resistor

## Security Considerations
- **API Keys**: Store securely, use HTTPS endpoints
- **Data Privacy**: Avoid sensitive data in payloads
- **Network Security**: Use WPA2/WPA3 networks
- **Access Control**: Limit webhook endpoint access
- **Authentication**: Use bearer tokens or API keys

## Use Cases
- **Home Automation**: Trigger smart home routines
- **Industrial Monitoring**: Send alerts to monitoring systems
- **Security Systems**: Integrate with security services
- **Environmental Monitoring**: Send data to cloud platforms
- **IoT Integration**: Connect to custom dashboards
- **Alert Systems**: Send notifications to messaging platforms
